mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where unemployment rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 60-day horizons or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks and corporate earnings pressure, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with industrial production declines over a 5-year sample period (p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 3.5 percent annually forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of months where CPI exceeds the 3.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces a negative information coefficient over rolling 60-day windows or fails to predict return direction with greater than 55% accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic expansion that translates into higher corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show zero or negative correlation with GDP growth rates over a 5-year sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production drop exceeding 1.0 percent signals acute manufacturing weakness and immediate earnings downgrades, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in more than 70% of weeks following industrial production declines greater than 1.0 percent.
